title: The Magicians of Caprona - Chapter 7 (Fragment 2)
text:
“We’re at war already,” said Old Niccolo. “Go away.”

“I regret,” said the Chief of Police, “that that is impossible.”

“Then don’t say you weren’t warned,” said Guido.

There was a short burst of song from the adults of both families. Paolo wished he knew that spell. It sounded useful. As soon as it was over, Rinaldo and a swarthy young Petrocchi came over to the two policemen and towed them away backwards. They were as stiff as the tailor’s dummies in the barred windows of Grossi’s. Rinaldo and the other young man laid them against the steps of the Art Gallery and returned each to his family, without looking at one another. As for the rest of the Ducal Police, they seemed to have vanished, bicycles and all.

“Ready now?” said Guido.

“Ready,” said Antonio.

And the single combat commenced.

Looking back on it afterwards, Paolo realized that it could not have lasted more than three minutes, though it seemed endless at the time. For, in that time, the strength, skill and speed of both champions was tried to the utmost. The first, and prob ably the longest, part was when the two were testing one another for an opening, and comparatively little seemed to happen. Both stood, leaning slightly forward, muttering, humming, occasionally flicking a hand. Paolo stared at his father’s strained face and wondered just what was going on. Then, momentarily, Guido was a man-shaped red-and-white check duster. Someone gasped. But Antonio almost simultaneously became a cardboard man covered with green triangles. Then both flicked back to themselves again.

The speed of it astounded Paolo. A spell had not only been cast on both sides, but also a counter-spell, and a spell counter to that, all in the time it took someone to gasp. Both combatants were panting and looking warily at each other. It was clear they were very evenly matched.

Again there was a space when nothing seemed to happen, except a sort of flickering on both sides. Then suddenly Antonio struck, and struck so hard that it was plain he had all the time been building a strong spell, beneath the flicker of trivial spells designed to keep Guido occupied. Guido gave a shout and dissolved into dust, which swept away back wards in a spiral. But, somehow, as he dissolved, he threw his strong spell at Antonio. Antonio broke into a thousand little pieces, like a spilled jigsaw puzzle.

For an ageless time, the swirl of dust and the pile of broken Antonio hung in midair. Both were struggling to stay together and not to patter down on the uprooted cobbles of the Corso. In fact, they were still struggling to make spells too. When, at last, Antonio staggered forward in one piece, holding some kind of red fruit in his right hand, he had barely time to dodge. Guido was a leopard in mid-spring.

Elizabeth screamed.

Antonio threw himself to one side, heaved a breath and sang. “Oliphans!” His usually silky voice was rough and ragged, but he hit the right notes. A gigantic elephant, with tusks longer than Paolo was tall, cut off the low sun and shook the Corso as it advanced, ears spread, to trample the attacking leopard. It was hard to believe the great beast was indeed worried, thin Antonio Montana.

For a shadow of a second, the leopard was Guido Petrocchi, very white in the face and luridly red in the beard, gabbling a frantic song. “Hickory-dickory-muggery mus!” And he must have hit the right notes too. He seemed to vanish.

vocabulary.definition: Surprise or shock someone greatly.
vocabulary.example: The speed of it astounded Paolo.

vocabulary.definition: A sudden outbreak or explosion.
vocabulary.example: There was a short burst of song from the adults of both families.

vocabulary.definition: Begin, start.
vocabulary.example: And the single combat commenced.

vocabulary.definition: A spell used to neutralize or counteract another spell.
vocabulary.example: A spell had not only been cast on both sides, but also a counter-spell, and a spell counter to that, all in the time it took someone to gasp.

vocabulary.definition: Become or cause to become liquid; fade away or disappear.
vocabulary.example: Guido gave a shout and dissolved into dust, which swept away back wards in a spiral.

vocabulary.definition: Avoid by a sudden quick movement.
vocabulary.example: When, at last, Antonio staggered forward in one piece, holding some kind of red fruit in his right hand, he had barely time to dodge.

vocabulary.definition: Move or shine unsteadily; vary rapidly in brightness.
vocabulary.example: Again there was a space when nothing seemed to happen, except a sort of flickering on both sides.

vocabulary.definition: Wild or distraught with fear, anxiety, or other emotion.
vocabulary.example: For a shadow of a second, the leopard was Guido Petrocchi, very white in the face and luridly red in the beard, gabbling a frantic song.

vocabulary.definition: Talk rapidly and unintelligibly.
vocabulary.example: For a shadow of a second, the leopard was Guido Petrocchi, very white in the face and luridly red in the beard, gabbling a frantic song.

vocabulary.definition: Of very great size or extent; huge.
vocabulary.example: A gigantic elephant, with tusks longer than Paolo was tall, cut off the low sun and shook the Corso as it advanced, ears spread, to trample the attacking leopard.

vocabulary.definition: Lift or haul (a heavy thing) with great effort.
vocabulary.example: Antonio threw himself to one side, heaved a breath and sang.

vocabulary.definition: Not able to occur, exist, or be done.
vocabulary.example:
”

“I regret,” said the Chief of Police, “that that is impossible.

vocabulary.definition: Say something in a low or barely audible voice.
vocabulary.example: Both stood, leaning slightly forward, muttering, humming, occasionally flicking a hand.

vocabulary.definition: A space or gap that allows access or passage; a chance for action.
vocabulary.example: The first, and prob ably the longest, part was when the two were testing one another for an opening, and comparatively little seemed to happen.

vocabulary.definition: A game or problem designed to test ingenuity or knowledge.
vocabulary.example: Antonio broke into a thousand little pieces, like a spilled jigsaw puzzle.

vocabulary.definition: Having a rough, irregular, or uneven surface or outline.
vocabulary.example: ” His usually silky voice was rough and ragged, but he hit the right notes.

vocabulary.definition: Feel sad, repentant, or disappointed over something that has happened or been done.
vocabulary.example:
”

“I regret,” said the Chief of Police, “that that is impossible.

vocabulary.definition: A curve which emanates from a point, moving progressively farther away as it revolves around the point.
vocabulary.example: Guido gave a shout and dissolved into dust, which swept away back wards in a spiral.

vocabulary.definition: Make forceful or violent efforts to get free or overcome difficulty.
vocabulary.example: Both were struggling to stay together and not to patter down on the uprooted cobbles of the Corso.

vocabulary.definition: Take measures to check the quality, performance, or reliability of something.
vocabulary.example: The first, and prob ably the longest, part was when the two were testing one another for an opening, and comparatively little seemed to happen.

vocabulary.definition: Pull (a vehicle or boat) along with a rope or chain.
vocabulary.example: As soon as it was over, Rinaldo and a swarthy young Petrocchi came over to the two policemen and towed them away backwards.

vocabulary.definition: Step heavily on something so as to crush or harm it.
vocabulary.example: A gigantic elephant, with tusks longer than Paolo was tall, cut off the low sun and shook the Corso as it advanced, ears spread, to trample the attacking leopard.

vocabulary.definition: Most extreme; greatest.
vocabulary.example: For, in that time, the strength, skill and speed of both champions was tried to the utmost.

vocabulary.definition: Disappear suddenly and completely.
vocabulary.example: As for the rest of the Ducal Police, they seemed to have vanished, bicycles and all.
